title: proptest-list-ready-monotone: closing/opening preserves list_ready monotonicity
body:
# Goal

Property 4 from the `df74809` follow-up list. Assert that closing an
issue can only ADD members to the next `list_ready` result; opening one
can only REMOVE. Cycle membership is the dual: an issue locked in a dep
cycle should NEVER appear in `list_ready`, regardless of dep-kind mix.

This is the property that would have falsified `121f48b` (mixed-kind
blocks+parent-child cycle silently accepted, locking both issues out of
`jjf ready`) directly.

# Approach

- Use `multi_issue_repo_strategy` (from `proptest-multi-issue-generator`) to spin up N issues with random dep edges.
- Snapshot `list_ready()` before and after each mutation; assert the symmetric-difference direction matches the mutation's expected effect (closing → fresh members only added; opening → members only removed; status-preserving verbs → identical result).
- For cycle-locking: when `add_dep_edge` lands successfully (post-rejection check), the issues involved must remain reachable from `list_ready` IF the kind set still permits readiness, and must NOT appear if a new cycle would close. (After `0f2fb9db` lands cycle-rejection at write time, the cycle path is mostly defensive; still worth asserting.)

# Why now

`list_ready` is the headline agent primitive. A regression here means agents see a wrong "next task" — high-leverage bug class.

# Blocked by

- `proptest-multi-issue-generator` (needs multi-stage generator).

# References

- Parent: `df74809` (qa-proptest-harness), commit `28e5ed71`.
- Bug this would have caught: `121f48b` (mixed-kind cycle).
- Cycle-rejection fix: commit `0f2fb9db`.

# Acceptance

- New property `list_ready_monotone_under_status_flips` ships green at 16 cases.
- Stress-runs clean at `PROPTEST_CASES=256`.
